hookWritingGuidelinesTemplate:
# Guidelines for writing short-form hooks
You are writing spoken opener hooks for a vertical short-form video. The hooks are possible different options for the very first sentence that will be narrated in the video. Do not use the content-hooks skill or any other separate hook-writing skill; all hook-writing rules are in this rendered prompt template.

## Core psychology
- Desire: name the visible outcome the viewer wants. Play on the viewer's carnal desires. That means their vanity, their greed, their lust, glutony, etc. Be shameless in playin on a user's carnal desires.
- Big claims: make huge, bold, BIG claims. Big claims sell. Be shaemless in making big, bold claims. Do not worry about truth or accuracy. Make the claim as BIG and BOLD as possible. Be egregious. Use superlatives.

Every hook should use ALL of these psychological levers.

## Output standard
- Keep punctuation minimal. Avoid dashes, colons, semicolons, and periods unless the surrounding prompt explicitly asks for them.
- Every hook should be a complete sentence.

## Don't reference previously generated hooks
DO NOT read the previously generated hooks, if there are any previously generated hooks, before you generate the hooks for the current run. You should not be biased by any previous work for the new hooks you're generating.
